Such term also includes any property (not otherwise a real estate asset) attributable to the temporary investment of new capital, but only if such property is stock or a debt instrument, and only for the 1-year period beginning on the date the real estate trust receives such capital.

children.label: E
children.text: A regular or residual interest in a REMIC shall be treated as a real estate asset, and any amount includible in gross income with respect to such an interest shall be treated as interest on an obligation secured by a mortgage on real property; except that, if less than 95 percent of the assets of such REMIC are real estate assets (determined as if the real estate investment trust held such assets), such real estate investment trust shall be treated as holding directly (and as receiving directly) its proportionate share of the assets and income of the REMIC. For purposes of determining whether any interest in a REMIC qualifies under the preceding sentence, any interest held by such REMIC in another REMIC shall be treated as a real estate asset under principles similar to the principles of the preceding sentence, except that, if such REMIC’s are part of a tiered structure, they shall be treated as one REMIC for purposes of this subparagraph.

children.label: I .—Timber real estate investment trust
children.text: The term “timber real estate investment trust” means a real estate investment trust in which more than 50 percent in value of its total assets consists of real property held in connection with the trade or business of producing timber.

children.heading: .—Administrative provisions
children.label: iii .—Administrative provisions
children.text: For purposes of subtitle F, the taxes imposed by this subparagraph shall be treated as excise taxes with respect to which the deficiency procedures of such subtitle apply.

children.heading: .—Election after tax-free reorganization
children.label: 8 .—Election after tax-free reorganization
children.text: If a corporation was a distributing corporation or a controlled corporation (other than a controlled corporation with respect to a distribution described in section 355(h)(2)(A)) with respect to any distribution to which section 355 (or so much of section 356 as relates to section 355) applied, such corporation (and any successor corporation) shall not be eligible to make any election under paragraph (1) for any taxable year beginning before the end of the 10-year period beginning on the date of such distribution.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Personal property shall be treated as a real estate asset for purposes of paragraph (4)(A) to the extent that rents attributable to such personal property are treated as rents from real property under subsection (d)(1)(C).

children.label: ii Determination of fair market value.—

children.text: Except as provided in subclause (II), the fair market value of all such property shall be determined for purposes of clause (i) in the same manner as the fair market value of real property is determined for purposes of apportioning interest income between real property and personal property under paragraph (3)(B).

children.heading: .—Gain on disposition
children.label: II .—Gain on disposition
children.text: For purposes of applying clause (i)(III), fair market value shall be determined at the time of sale or other disposition.

children.heading: .—Termination date
children.label: 10 .—Termination date
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “termination date” means, with respect to any taxpayer, the last day of the taxpayer’s first taxable year beginning after the date of the enactment of this paragraph and before the date that is 1 year after such date of enactment.

children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the amount treated as received for any service (or management or operation) shall not be less than 150 percent of the direct cost of the trust in furnishing or rendering the service (or providing the management or operation).

children.text: For purposes of paragraphs (2) and (3) of subsection (c), amounts described in subparagraph (A) shall be included in the gross income of the corporation, trust, or association.

children.text: The term “lodging facility” includes customary amenities and facilities operated as part of, or associated with, the lodging facility so long as such amenities and facilities are customary for other properties of a comparable size and class owned by other owners unrelated to such real estate investment trust.

children.text: References in this paragraph to operating a property shall be treated as including a reference to managing the property.

children.text: Persons shall be treated as related to each other if such persons are treated as a single employer under subsection (a) or (b) of section 52.

children.heading: Special rules for foreclosure property
children.label: e Special rules for foreclosure property

children.heading: Foreclosure property defined
children.label: 1 Foreclosure property defined
children.text: For purposes of this part, the term “foreclosure property” means any real property (including interests in real property), and any personal property incident to such real property, acquired by the real estate investment trust as the result of such trust having bid in such property at foreclosure, or having otherwise reduced such property to ownership or possession by agreement or process of law, after there was default (or default was imminent) on a lease of such property or on an indebtedness which such property secured. Such term does not include property acquired by the real estate investment trust as a result of indebtedness arising from the sale or other disposition of property of the trust described in section 1221(a)(1) which was not originally acquired as foreclosure property.

children.heading: Grace period
children.label: 2 Grace period
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3), property shall cease to be foreclosure property with respect to the real estate investment trust as of the close of the 3d taxable year following the taxable year in which the trust acquired such property.

children.heading: Extensions
children.label: 3 Extensions
children.text: If the real estate investment trust establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary that an extension of the grace period is necessary for the orderly liquidation of the trust’s interests in such property, the Secretary may grant one extension of the grace period for such property. Any such extension shall not extend the grace period beyond the close of the 3d taxable year following the last taxable year in the period under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Termination of grace period in certain cases
children.label: 4 Termination of grace period in certain cases
A
children.text: a lease is entered into with respect to such property which, by its terms, will give rise to income which is not described in subsection (c)(3) (other than subparagraph (F) of such subsection), or any amount is received or accrued, directly or indirectly, pursuant to a lease entered into on or after such day which is not described in such subsection,

children.label: B
children.text: any construction takes place on such property (other than completion of a building, or completion of any other improvement, where more than 10 percent of the construction of such building or other improvement was completed before default became imminent), or

children.label: C
children.text: if such day is more than 90 days after the day on which such property was acquired by the real estate investment trust and the property is used in a trade or business which is conducted by the trust (other than through an independent contractor (within the meaning of section (d)(3)) from whom the trust itself does not derive or receive any income or through a taxable REIT subsidiary).

children.heading: Taxpayer must make election
children.label: 5 Taxpayer must make election
children.text: Property shall be treated as foreclosure property for purposes of this part only if the real estate investment trust so elects (in the manner provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary) on or before the due date (including any extensions of time) for filing its return of tax under this chapter for the taxable year in which such trust acquires such property. A real estate investment trust may revoke any such election for a taxable year by filing the revocation (in the manner provided by the Secretary) on or before the due date (including any extension of time) for filing its return of tax under this chapter for the taxable year. If a trust revokes an election for any property, no election may be made by the trust under this paragraph with respect to the property for any subsequent taxable year.

children.heading: Health care facility
children.label: ii Health care facility
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term “health care facility” means a hospital, nursing facility, assisted living facility, congregate care facility, qualified continuing care facility (as defined in section 7872(g)(4)), or other licensed facility which extends medical or nursing or ancillary services to patients and which, immediately before the termination, expiration, default, or breach of the lease of or mortgage secured by such facility, was operated by a provider of such services which was eligible for participation in the medicare program under title XVIII of the Social Security Act with respect to such facility.

children.text: any amount so received or accrued shall not be excluded from the term “interest” solely by reason of being based on a fixed percentage or percentages of receipts or sales, and

children.label: B
children.text: where a real estate investment trust receives any amount which would be excluded from the term “interest” solely because the debtor of the real estate investment trust receives or accrues any amount the determination of which depends in whole or in part on the income or profits of any person, only a proportionate part (determined pursuant to regulations prescribed by the Secretary) of the amount received or accrued by the real estate investment trust from the debtor will be excluded from the term “interest”.

children.label: A
children.text: a real estate investment trust receives or accrues with respect to an obligation secured by a mortgage on real property or an interest in real property amounts from a debtor which derives substantially all of its gross income with respect to such property (not taking into account any gain on any disposition) from the leasing of substantially all of its interests in such property to tenants, and

children.label: B
children.text: a portion of the amount which such debtor receives or accrues, directly or indirectly, from tenants consists of qualified rents (as defined in subsection (d)(6)(B)),

children.heading: Failure to qualify
children.label: 1 Failure to qualify
children.text: An election under subsection (c)(1) made by a corporation, trust, or association shall terminate if the corporation, trust, or association is not a real estate investment trust to which the provisions of this part apply for the taxable year with respect to which the election is made, or for any succeeding taxable year unless paragraph (5) applies. Such termination shall be effective for the taxable year for which the corporation, trust, or association is not a real estate investment trust to which the provisions of this part apply, and for all succeeding taxable years.

children.heading: Revocation
children.label: 2 Revocation
children.text: An election under subsection (c)(1) made by a corporation, trust, or association may be revoked by it for any taxable year after the first taxable year for which the election is effective. A revocation under this paragraph shall be effective for the taxable year in which made and for all succeeding taxable years. Such revocation must be made on or before the 90th day after the first day of the first taxable year for which the revocation is to be effective. Such revocation shall be made in such manner as the Secretary shall prescribe by regulations.

children.heading: Election after termination or revocation
children.label: 3 Election after termination or revocation
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (4), if a corporation, trust, or association has made an election under subsection (c)(1) and such election has been terminated or revoked under paragraph (1) or paragraph (2), such corporation, trust, or association (and any successor corporation, trust, or association) shall not be eligible to make an election under subsection (c)(1) for any taxable year prior to the fifth taxable year which begins after the first taxable year for which such termination or revocation is effective.

children.label: A
children.text: the corporation, trust, or association does not willfully fail to file within the time prescribed by law an income tax return for the taxable year with respect to which the termination of the election under subsection (c)(1) occurs;

children.label: B
children.text: the inclusion of any incorrect information in the return referred to in subparagraph (A) is not due to fraud with intent to evade tax; and

children.label: C
children.text: the corporation, trust, or association establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary that its failure to qualify as a real estate investment trust to which the provisions of this part apply is due to reasonable cause and not due to willful neglect.

children.label: A
children.text: which is not a real estate investment trust to which the provisions of this part apply for the taxable year due to one or more failures to comply with one or more of the provisions of this part (other than paragraph (2), (3), or (4) of subsection (c)),

children.label: B
children.text: such failures are due to reasonable cause and not due to willful neglect, and

children.label: C
children.text: if such corporation, trust, or association pays (as prescribed by the Secretary in regulations and in the same manner as tax) a penalty of $50,000 for each failure to satisfy a provision of this part due to reasonable cause and not willful neglect.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a)(6), a corporation, trust, or association is closely held if the stock ownership requirement of section 542(a)(2) is met.

children.heading: Waiver of partnership attribution, etc.
children.label: B Waiver of partnership attribution, etc.
i
children.text: paragraph (2) of section 544(a) shall be applied as if such paragraph did not contain the phrase “or by or for his partner”, and

children.label: ii
children.text: sections 544(a)(4)(A) and 544(b)(1) shall be applied by substituting “the entity meet the stock ownership requirement of section 542(a)(2)” for “the corporation a personal holding company”.

children.heading: Subsections (a)(5) and (6) not to apply to 1st year
children.label: 2 Subsections (a)(5) and (6) not to apply to 1st year
children.text: Paragraphs (5) and (6) of subsection (a) shall not apply to the 1st taxable year for which an election is made under subsection (c)(1) by any corporation, trust, or association.

children.heading: Treatment of trusts described in section 401(a)
children.label: 3 Treatment of trusts described in section 401(a)

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), in determining whether the stock ownership requirement of section 542(a)(2) is met for purposes of paragraph (1)(A), any stock held by a qualified trust shall be treated as held directly by its beneficiaries in proportion to their actuarial interests in such trust and shall not be treated as held by such trust.

children.heading: Certain related trusts not eligible
children.label: ii Certain related trusts not eligible
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to any qualified trust if one or more disqualified persons (as defined in section 4975(e)(2), without regard to subparagraphs (B) and (I) thereof) with respect to such qualified trust hold in the aggregate 5 percent or more in value of the interests in the real estate investment trust and such real estate investment trust has accumulated earnings and profits attributable to any period for which it did not qualify as a real estate investment trust.

children.heading: Coordination with personal holding company rules
children.label: B Coordination with personal holding company rules
children.text: If any entity qualifies as a real estate investment trust for any taxable year by reason of subparagraph (A), such entity shall not be treated as a personal holding company for such taxable year for purposes of part II of subchapter G of this chapter.

children.heading: Treatment for purposes of unrelated business tax
children.label: C Treatment for purposes of unrelated business tax
i
children.text: the gross income (less direct expenses related thereto) of the REIT for the REIT year from unrelated trades or businesses (determined as if the REIT were a qualified trust), bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the gross income (less direct expenses related thereto) of the REIT for the REIT year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A real estate investment trust is a pension-held REIT if such trust would not have qualified as a real estate investment trust but for the provisions of this paragraph and if such trust is predominantly held by qualified trusts.

children.label: I
children.text: at least 1 qualified trust holds more than 25 percent (by value) of the interests in such real estate investment trust, or

children.label: II
children.text: 1 or more qualified trusts (each of whom own more than 10 percent by value of the interests in such real estate investment trust) hold in the aggregate more than 50 percent (by value) of the interests in such real estate investment trust.

children.heading: Qualified trust
children.label: E Qualified trust
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “qualified trust” means any trust described in section 401(a) and exempt from tax under section 501(a).

children.heading: Treatment of certain wholly owned subsidiaries
children.label: i Treatment of certain wholly owned subsidiaries
A
children.text: a corporation which is a qualified REIT subsidiary shall not be treated as a separate corporation, and

children.label: B
children.text: all assets, liabilities, and items of income, deduction, and credit of a qualified REIT subsidiary shall be treated as assets, liabilities, and such items (as the case may be) of the real estate investment trust.

children.heading: Qualified REIT subsidiary
children.label: 2 Qualified REIT subsidiary
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “qualified REIT subsidiary” means any corporation if 100 percent of the stock of such corporation is held by the real estate investment trust. Such term shall not include a taxable REIT subsidiary.

children.heading: Treatment of termination of qualified subsidiary status
children.label: 3 Treatment of termination of qualified subsidiary status
children.text: For purposes of this subtitle, if any corporation which was a qualified REIT subsidiary ceases to meet the requirements of paragraph (2), such corporation shall be treated as a new corporation acquiring all of its assets (and assuming all of its liabilities) immediately before such cessation from the real estate investment trust in exchange for its stock.

children.heading: Treatment of shared appreciation mortgages
children.label: j Treatment of shared appreciation mortgages

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Solely for purposes of subsection (c) of this section and section 857(b)(6), any income derived from a shared appreciation provision shall be treated as gain recognized on the sale of the secured property.

children.label: A
children.text: the real estate investment trust shall be treated as holding the secured property for the period during which it held the shared appreciation provision (or, if shorter, for the period during which the secured property was held by the person holding such property), and

children.label: B
children.text: the secured property shall be treated as property described in section 1221(a)(1) if it is so described in the hands of the person holding the secured property (or it would be so described if held by the real estate investment trust).

children.heading: Coordination with prohibited transactions safe harbor
children.label: 3 Coordination with prohibited transactions safe harbor
A
children.text: the real estate investment trust shall be treated as having sold the secured property when it recognizes any income described in paragraph (1), and

children.label: B
children.text: any expenditures made by any holder of the secured property shall be treated as made by the real estate investment trust.

children.label: i
children.text: the secured property is sold or otherwise disposed of pursuant to a case under title 11 of the United States Code,

children.label: ii
children.text: the seller is under the jurisdiction of the court in such case, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the disposition is required by the court or is pursuant to a plan approved by the court.

children.label: i
children.text: the secured property was acquired by the seller with the intent to evict or foreclose, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the trust knew or had reason to know that default on the obligation described in paragraph (5)(A) would occur.

children.label: i
children.text: which is in connection with an obligation which is held by the real estate investment trust and is secured by an interest in real property, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which entitles the real estate investment trust to receive a specified portion of any gain realized on the sale or exchange of such real property (or of any gain which would be realized if the property were sold on a specified date) or appreciation in value as of any specified date.

children.heading: Secured property
children.label: B Secured property
children.text: The term “secured property” means the real property referred to in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Requirement that entity not be closely held treated as met in certain cases
children.label: k Requirement that entity not be closely held treated as met in certain cases

children.text: which for a taxable year meets the requirements of section 857(f)(1), and
which does not know, or exercising reasonable diligence would not have known, whether the entity failed to meet the requirement of subsection (a)(6),

children.label: A
children.text: such trust directly or indirectly owns stock in such corporation, and

children.label: B
children.text: such trust and such corporation jointly elect that such corporation shall be treated as a taxable REIT subsidiary of such trust for purposes of this part.

children.heading: Thirty-five percent ownership in another taxable REIT subsidiary
children.label: 2 Thirty-five percent ownership in another taxable REIT subsidiary
A
children.text: securities possessing more than 35 percent of the total voting power of the outstanding securities of such corporation, or

children.label: B
children.text: securities having a value of more than 35 percent of the total value of the outstanding securities of such corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: any corporation which directly or indirectly operates or manages a lodging facility or a health care facility, and

children.label: B
children.text: any corporation which directly or indirectly provides to any other person (under a franchise, license, or otherwise) rights to any brand name under which any lodging facility or health care facility is operated.

children.heading: Lodging facility
children.label: A Lodging facility
children.text: The term “lodging facility” has the meaning given to such term by subsection (d)(9)(D)(ii).

children.heading: Health care facility
children.label: B Health care facility
children.text: The term “health care facility” has the meaning given to such term by subsection (e)(6)(D)(ii).

children.heading: Safe harbor in applying subsection (c)(4)
children.label: m Safe harbor in applying subsection (c)(4)
A
children.text: Straight debt securities of an issuer which meet the requirements of paragraph (2).

children.label: B
children.text: Any loan to an individual or an estate.

children.label: C
children.text: Any section 467 rental agreement (as defined in section 467(d)), other than with a person described in subsection (d)(2)(B).

children.label: D
children.text: Any obligation to pay rents from real property (as defined in subsection (d)(1)).

children.label: E
children.text: Any security issued by a State or any political subdivision thereof, the District of Columbia, a foreign government or any political subdivision thereof, or the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, but only if the determination of any payment received or accrued under such security does not depend in whole or in part on the profits of any entity not described in this subparagraph or payments on any obligation issued by such an entity,

children.label: F
children.text: Any security issued by a real estate investment trust.

children.label: G
children.text: Any other arrangement as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Special rules relating to straight debt securities
children.label: 2 Special rules relating to straight debt securities

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1)(A), securities meet the requirements of this paragraph if such securities are straight debt, as defined in section 1361(c)(5) (without regard to subparagraph (B)(iii) thereof).

children.heading: Special rules relating to certain contingencies
children.label: B Special rules relating to certain contingencies
I
children.text: any such contingency does not have the effect of changing the effective yield to maturity, as determined under section 1272, other than a change in the annual yield to maturity which does not exceed the greater of ¼ of 1 percent or 5 percent of the annual yield to maturity, or

children.label: II
children.text: neither the aggregate issue price nor the aggregate face amount of the issuer’s debt instruments held by the trust exceeds $1,000,000 and not more than 12 months of unaccrued interest can be required to be prepaid thereunder, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the time or amount of payment is subject to a contingency upon a default or the exercise of a prepayment right by the issuer of the debt, but only if such contingency is consistent with customary commercial practice.

children.heading: Special rules relating to corporate or partnership issuers
children.label: C Special rules relating to corporate or partnership issuers
i
children.text: are not described in paragraph (1) (prior to the application of this subparagraph), and

children.label: ii
children.text: have an aggregate value greater than 1 percent of the issuer’s outstanding securities determined without regard to paragraph (3)(A)(i).

children.label: i
children.text: a trust’s interest as a partner in a partnership (as defined in section 7701(a)(2)) shall not be considered a security, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the trust shall be deemed to own its proportionate share of each of the assets of the partnership.

children.heading: Determination of trust’s interest in partnership assets
children.label: B Determination of trust’s interest in partnership assets
i
children.text: the trust’s interest in the partnership assets shall be the trust’s proportionate interest in any securities issued by the partnership (determined without regard to subparagraph (A)(i) and paragraph (4), but not including securities described in paragraph (1)), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the value of any debt instrument shall be the adjusted issue price thereof, as defined in section 1272(a)(4).

children.heading: Certain partnership debt instruments not treated as a security
children.label: 4 Certain partnership debt instruments not treated as a security
A
children.text: any debt instrument issued by a partnership and not described in paragraph (1) shall not be considered a security to the extent of the trust’s interest as a partner in the partnership, and

children.label: B
children.text: any debt instrument issued by a partnership and not described in paragraph (1) shall not be considered a security if at least 75 percent of the partnership’s gross income (excluding gross income from prohibited transactions) is derived from sources referred to in subsection (c)(3).

children.heading: Secretarial guidance
children.label: 5 Secretarial guidance
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to provide guidance (including through the issuance of a written determination, as defined in section 6110(b)) that an arrangement shall not be considered a security held by the trust for purposes of applying subclause (III) of subsection (c)(4)(B)(iv) notwithstanding that such arrangement otherwise could be considered a security under subparagraph (F) of subsection (c)(5).

children.heading: Rules regarding foreign currency transactions
children.label: n Rules regarding foreign currency transactions
A
children.text: passive foreign exchange gain for any taxable year shall not constitute gross income for purposes of subsection (c)(2), and

children.label: B
children.text: real estate foreign exchange gain for any taxable year shall not constitute gross income for purposes of subsection (c)(3).

children.label: i
children.text: any item of income or gain described in subsection (c)(3),

children.label: ii
children.text: the acquisition or ownership of obligations secured by mortgages on real property or on interests in real property (other than foreign currency gain attributable to any item of income or gain described in clause (i)), or

children.label: iii
children.text: becoming or being the obligor under obligations secured by mortgages on real property or on interests in real property (other than foreign currency gain attributable to any item of income or gain described in clause (i)),

children.label: i
children.text: subsection (c)(3) for the taxable year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: subsection (c)(4)(A) at the close of each quarter that the real estate investment trust has directly or indirectly held the qualified business unit, and

children.label: C
children.text: any other foreign currency gain as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: real estate foreign exchange gain,

children.label: i
children.text: any item of income or gain described in subsection (c)(2),

children.label: ii
children.text: the acquisition or ownership of obligations (other than foreign currency gain attributable to any item of income or gain described in clause (i)), or

children.label: iii
children.text: becoming or being the obligor under obligations (other than foreign currency gain attributable to any item of income or gain described in clause (i)), and

children.label: C
children.text: any other foreign currency gain as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Exception for income from substantial and regular trading
children.label: 4 Exception for income from substantial and regular trading
children.text: Notwithstanding this subsection or any other provision of this part, any section 988 gain derived by a corporation, trust, or association from dealing, or engaging in substantial and regular trading, in securities (as defined in section 475(c)(2)) shall constitute gross income which does not qualify under paragraph (2) or (3) of subsection (c). This paragraph shall not apply to income which does not constitute gross income by reason of subsection (c)(5)(G).
